chunks.text: This 2025 Supreme Court entry is tagged for IPC Section 420 research, latest-case retrieval, citation validation, court filtering, and paragraph-level source display. It demonstrates how the assistant should surface the newest indexed authority without inventing a judgment.

A Section 420 IPC result must preserve case title, citation, court, year, judge or bench, paragraph locator, and source link. If these fields are missing, the answer must be blocked or marked insufficient rather than completed from memory.

documents.title: CGST Act, 2017 — Section 16: Input Tax Credit Eligibility and Conditions
documents.authority_status: Statutory provision as amended by Finance Act, 2023. In force.
treatment_summary: Amended by Finance Act, 2023 — restrictions on ITC for non-filing of returns
Linked notifications: 49/2019, 13/2022, 22/2023

related_products.title: CGST Act with Rules and Notifications

chunks.topic: Eligibility and conditions for input tax credit
chunks.text: Section 16 of the CGST Act, 2017 provides that every registered person shall be entitled to input tax credit on supply of goods or services used in the course or furtherance of business. Sub-section (2) prescribes conditions including possession of tax invoice, receipt of goods, and payment of tax to the supplier.

chunks.topic: Input tax credit conditions and restrictions
chunks.text: Queries about GST input tax credit and Section 16 should retrieve sources carrying act, section, topic, year, and source metadata. The answer layer must not create a tax rule from memory; it may only quote or summarize indexed evidence and cite the source marker.

documents.title: Vodafone India Ltd vs Union of India
documents.judge: Justice S. Kapadia, Justice K.S. Radhakrishnan
documents.authority_status: Reported in GST Cases. Binding precedent.
treatment_summary: Applied in 2025: Bharti Airtel vs. Department of Revenue
Followed in 2025: Idea Cellular vs. State of Gujarat

chunks.text: The petitioner Vodafone India Ltd challenged the denial of input tax credit under Section 16 of the CGST Act on the ground that the supplier had not filed returns. The Supreme Court held that the substantive right to input tax credit cannot be denied solely due to procedural non-compliance by the supplier when the recipient had acted in good faith and satisfied all other conditions.

The Supreme Court interpreted Section 16(2) of the CGST Act and held that the conditions of possessing tax invoice, receiving goods, and paying tax to the supplier are substantive, while the supplier's filing of returns is procedural. The Court allowed the appeal and directed the department to grant ITC subject to verification of genuine transaction.

documents.title: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy (Retd.) vs Union of India — Right to Privacy
documents.judge: Chief Justice J.S. Khehar, Justice J. Chelameswar, Justice S.A. Bobde, Justice R.K. Agrawal, Justice R.F. Nariman, Justice A.M. Sapre, Justice D.Y. Chandrachud, Justice S.K. Kaul, Justice S. Abdul Nazeer
documents.publisher: Supreme Court of India / Eastern Book Company
documents.authority_status: Landmark judgment. Binding precedent. Most authoritative on privacy.
treatment_summary: Overruled: M.P. Sharma vs. Satish Chandra (1954)
Overruled: Kharak Singh vs. State of UP (1963)
Followed in 2019: Aadhaar judgment
Followed in 2023: Data Protection Act framework

chunks.topic: Right to privacy as fundamental right
chunks.text: The nine-judge Constitution Bench unanimously held that the right to privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Constitution of India. The Court overruled previous decisions that had held otherwise. Privacy is an essential component of personal liberty and dignity, and it protects the autonomy of the individual over their body, mind, and personal data.

documents.title: Committee of Creditors of Essar Steel vs. Satish Kumar Gupta — Resolution Plan
documents.judge: Justice R.F. Nariman, Justice Aniruddha Bose, Justice V. Ramasubramanian
documents.topic: Resolution plan and committee of creditors
documents.publisher: Supreme Court of India / Eastern Book Company
documents.authority_status: Leading authority on IBC resolution plan. Binding precedent.
treatment_summary: Followed in 2021: ArcelorMittal India vs. Satish Kumar Gupta
Applied in 2022: NCLAT resolution plan appeals
Cited in 2023: IBC Amendment Act validation

chunks.topic: Resolution plan review by committee of creditors
chunks.text: The Supreme Court held that the commercial wisdom of the Committee of Creditors in approving or rejecting a resolution plan under Section 30 of the IBC is not justiciable. The adjudicating authority can only examine whether the plan meets the mandatory requirements of Section 30(2) and cannot substitute its own commercial judgment.

documents.title: Rohit Sharma vs State of Maharashtra
documents.judge: Justice A. Sharma, Justice P. Verma
documents.topic: Cheating, breach of trust, criminal appeal
documents.authority_status: Reported in SCC 2025. Good law, no negative treatment.
treatment_summary: Followed in 2025: State of Gujarat vs. Rajesh Kumar
Cited in 2026: Anil Patel vs. State of Karnataka

chunks.text: The appellant Rohit Sharma was convicted under Section 420 IPC by the Sessions Court for inducing the complainant to part with Rs 15 lakh on false promise of government contract. The High Court upheld the conviction. The Supreme Court examined the ingredients of cheating under Section 420 IPC and held that dishonest inducement from inception was established.

The Supreme Court held that for an offence under Section 420 IPC, the prosecution must prove: (1) dishonest inducement by the accused, (2) the person induced delivered property, and (3) the inducement was made intentionally. The Court found all ingredients satisfied and dismissed the appeal, affirming the sentence of 5 years.

documents.title: Sanjay Chandra vs. Central Bureau of Investigation — Bail under Section 439 CrPC
documents.judge: Justice H.L. Dattu, Justice C.K. Prasad
documents.publisher: Supreme Court of India / Eastern Book Company
documents.authority_status: Leading authority on bail jurisprudence. Good law.
treatment_summary: Followed in 2019: P. Chidambaram vs. CBI (bail matters)
Cited widely in bail jurisprudence across High Courts

related_products.title: Criminal Procedure Code: Practice and Procedure

chunks.text: The Supreme Court held that the primary factor in granting bail under Section 439 CrPC is the likelihood of the accused fleeing from justice or tampering with evidence. The seriousness of the offence alone is not sufficient to deny bail. The Court granted bail to the accused in the 2G spectrum case, emphasizing that bail is the rule and jail is the exception.

documents.title: EBC SCC Online Commentary: Input Tax Credit Section 16 CGST Act
documents.topic: Input tax credit — commentary and analysis
treatment_summary: Covers statutory text, amendments, judicial interpretations
Includes all relevant notifications up to Finance Act 2024

chunks.text: Section 16 of the CGST Act governs the eligibility and conditions for claiming input tax credit. The provision has been the subject of extensive litigation, particularly regarding the conditions for ITC under sub-section (2) and the restrictions under sub-section (4). The EBC editorial commentary provides a section-by-section analysis with case references.

documents.title: EBC Criminal Law Journal: Bail and Personal Liberty under Article 21
documents.topic: Bail, personal liberty, and Article 21 jurisprudence
treatment_summary: Analyzes bail jurisprudence under Article 21
Covers Sanjay Chandra and subsequent bail decisions

related_products.title: Criminal Procedure Code: Practice and Procedure

chunks.text: The right to bail is intrinsically linked to the right to personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution. The Supreme Court has consistently held that bail cannot be withheld as a punishment and that pre-trial detention must be justified by compelling reasons. The EBC Criminal Law Journal traces the evolution of bail jurisprudence from Gurbaksh Singh Sibbia to the present.
